cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to this question. So the story is about Mr. Williams who was driving and came across a crossroads with a "SLOW" sign. He slowed down, checked both ways, and then drove across without stopping completely. Then a policeman stopped him, saying he didn't stop at the crossing. Mr. Williams explained that the sign said "SLOW," not "STOP," and he did slow down. The policeman then realized he was in the wrong street. The question asks what Mr. Williams was doing when he was stopped by the policeman. The options are: A) was driving at a high speed B) was driving onto the main road C) was going to stop his car D) was driving slowly Let me go through the story again to make sure I get the details right. The key points here are the sign (SLOW, not STOP), Mr. Williams slowing down, looking both ways, and then driving across without stopping. The policeman accuses him of not stopping, but Mr. Williams says he slowed down because the sign said SLOW. The policeman then realizes he was in the wrong street, implying maybe he was supposed to be on a different road where the sign was different?
